articles.title: RUMOR: IPHONE 17 PRO COULD HAVE THREE UNEXPECTED CAMERA UPGRADES
articles.summary: A new anonymous tip claims the upcoming iPhone 17 Pro may feature major camera upgrades, including an 8x optical zoom lens, a pro camera app, and a new Camera Control button on the top edge, though these details remain unverified and speculative. Of the rumored features, the pro camera app seems most plausible, while hardware changes are more doubtful given Apple's typical leak patterns and past precedents.

articles.title: RANGE ROVER “RECODES DNA” WITH NEW WORDMARK AND MOTIF
articles.summary: Range Rover has unveiled a refined wordmark and a new visual motif as part of Jaguar Land Rover's broader strategy to create distinct identities for each of its four brands, aligning with a wider trend in the luxury automotive sector to modernize visual identities for a digital-first, younger, and more diverse audience. This shift reflects broader industry changes driven by the rise of electric vehicles and evolving consumer expectations, though critics warn that many rebrands risk losing brand distinctiveness in the pursuit of minimalism and modernity.

articles.title: OPENAI IS PREPARING SORA 2: AN AI MODEL WILL BE TAUGHT TO "SPEAK" IN VIDEOS
articles.summary: OpenAI is developing Sora 2, a significant upgrade to its text-to-video model that will integrate audio capabilities, including speech and environmental sounds. While the current Sora generates silent videos lasting over 20 seconds, Google's competing Veo 3 produces shorter clips with synchronized audio tracks. The audio-enabled competition between these AI video models could establish new creative standards while raising concerns about potential misinformation.

articles.title: "THE INDUSTRY NEEDS TO SLOW DOWN": A DAY IN THE LIFE OF ALI MARTÍN FILSOOF
articles.summary: Ali Martín Filsoof, founder of LA-based Design Dept, brings a multicultural perspective and minimalist aesthetic to branding, drawing from influences in Tehran, Lima, and Los Angeles as well as Japanese and Swiss design principles. His studio emphasizes strategic, culturally resonant, and crafted branding over fast-turnaround and template-driven work, valuing curiosity, depth, and purpose in every project, from luxury packaging to pro bono street vendor identities.

articles.title: DESIGN THINKING ISN'T DEAD – IT'S MORE CRUCIAL THAN EVER
articles.summary: While some in the U.S. claim that design thinking is dead, criticizing it as redundant or damaging, the approach continues to prove its value globally by emphasizing human insight, strategic problem-solving, and risk reduction in both commercial and public sectors. Rather than being obsolete, design thinking is evolving beyond screens and products to influence business, policy, and social impact, and it's business, not design, that risks falling behind if it fails to embrace this shift.

articles.title: AI WON'T KILL UX — WE WILL
articles.summary: While AI offers speed and efficiency, its reliance on past data and patterns risks standardizing design and stifling originality, especially when adopted uncritically at the center of the creative process. To preserve meaningful, human-centered innovation in UX/UI, we must intentionally integrate AI as a supportive tool, not a replacement, while reinvesting saved time into exploration, education, and inclusive, forward-thinking design.

articles.title: VISUALIZE WHAT MATTERS WITH AI
articles.summary: MyLens AI transforms your ideas and content into engaging visuals that are interactive, editable, and ready for presentation.

articles.title: TURN YOUR SLIDES INTO INTERACTIVE AI PRESENTATIONS
articles.summary: Jotform's AI Agents can present slides and answer live questions to keep users engaged. Simply upload existing slides or generate new ones with AI.

articles.title: THE SECRET TO GETTING REPEAT WORK AS A FREELANCER
articles.summary: When freelance work dries up, the best move isn't always chasing new clients, it's nurturing relationships with existing ones who already trust you and often provide the most rewarding projects. By staying in touch, being proactive, listening well, and occasionally just asking for work, freelancers can build long-term client partnerships that lead to more stability, more referrals, and ultimately, more fulfilling work.

articles.title: STOP SCROLLING: KINETIC TYPOGRAPHY IS REDEFINING UX
articles.summary: Kinetic typography (moving text) is redefining digital UX, transforming from mere eye candy into functional communication. Advanced front-end frameworks, AI design tools, and social media's motion-heavy aesthetic have made animated text more accessible and expected. When used thoughtfully, moving text directs attention, paces storytelling, and creates delightful interactions that make interfaces feel alive.

articles.title: THE DESIGNER'S GUIDE TO PROMPTS AND MODELS
articles.summary: Most designers write ineffective AI prompts, often requesting "beautiful buttons" without providing precise specifications or detailing every pixel unnecessarily. This guide recommends specific AI models based on work type, independence needs, quality requirements, and access preferences for context. Best practices include starting with design problems, providing context, leveraging AI beyond coding, and communicating design intent.

articles.title: I VIBE-CODED A DOG BIRTHDAY APP. WE STILL NEED DEVELOPERS
articles.summary: A former developer tested whether non-technical users could build functional apps using vibe coding platforms Firebase Studio and Lovable.

articles.title: LOVART AI IS THE NEW THREAT DESIGNERS DIDN'T NEED
articles.summary: Lovart AI is a $90-per-month design co-pilot that boasts award-winning creative team capabilities - it has attracted over 800,000 beta testers.
